name: Kornia (PyTorch Computer Vision)

performance.notes: PyTorch GPU acceleration; differentiable operations; batched processing

agent_readiness.notes: Differentiable computer vision library built on PyTorch. Image augmentation, geometry transforms, color operations, filtering, loss functions — all differentiable and GPU-accelerated. Batched operations on tensors. Geometric transforms (homography, affine, perspective). Depth estimation. Feature matching. Local features (DISK, LoFTR, DALF). Works end-to-end in PyTorch training pipelines. Particularly useful when augmentations need to be GPU-accelerated or differentiable.

gotchas: Less well-known than albumentations for augmentation
API can be complex for newcomers
Some features less mature than OpenCV equivalents
best_when: GPU-accelerated image augmentation in PyTorch training loops
Differentiable image processing (geometric transforms in loss functions)
Feature matching and homography estimation on GPU
PyTorch-native CV pipelines needing geometry/color operations
summary: Kornia is a differentiable computer vision library built on PyTorch. All operations (augmentation, geometry transforms, filtering, color) are differentiable and GPU-accelerated. Batched tensor operations. Feature matching (LoFTR, DISK). Best for PyTorch training pipelines needing GPU-accelerated or differentiable image operations. For simple augmentation, albumentations has a simpler API.
